summary.intent: Address and resolve multiple rounds of Codex review findings on PR #678 in fogodev/ars-ui, iterating through fix-commit, reply/resolve cycles, and re-triggering Codex review until approval.
summary.outcome: Two rounds of Codex review findings were addressed: 8 threads in the first pass (commit f9e1d504) and 4 threads in the second pass, with all threads replied to, resolved, and a follow-up commit pushed covering grid and splitter fixes.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot (@codex) for automated PR review that must be re-triggered after each fix push via a comment '@codex review'.
The review lifecycle requires: push fix → apply 'snapshot-reviewed' label → reply+resolve each Codex thread via GraphQL → trigger next Codex pass.
Tests are split across unit tests (--lib), spec conformance (--test spec_conformance), and property-based tests (--test proptest_state_machines) with the latter gated behind --ignored.
cargo insta is used for snapshot testing with --unreferenced=reject enforcement.
cargo xtask spec validate is part of the required check suite.
Grid stretch requires emitting align-items: stretch when no explicit align override is set.
Splitter initial controlled sizes must be normalized and clamped before entering context state.
Splitter handle aria-valuemax must use the right neighbor's effective minimum including collapsible collapsed_size.
SyncProps must clamp both normalized uncontrolled and controlled incoming sizes against current panel constraints.
Panel attributes must emit the id referenced by handle aria-controls.
Collapse transfer must respect the recipient neighbor max_size and partially collapse when capacity is limited.
Handle validation must use checked_add to prevent usize::MAX overflow.
Collapsible handle aria-valuemin must report the effective collapsed size including nonzero collapsed_size values.

code.finding: Grid auto_columns builder natural use path and stretch alignment emission needed targeted test coverage and fixes.
SyncProps invalid drag exit, full collapse with maxed recipients, and full dragging keymap all required fixes and new test coverage in this range.
TransitionPlan implementation inspected to validate state transition correctness for Splitter review findings.

workflow: Codex review threads can be resolved via GitHub GraphQL mutation resolveReviewThread with the thread ID.
PR comments are replied to via gh api POST to pulls/{n}/comments with in_reply_to parameter.
Codex approval is signaled by a +1 reaction; active review is signaled by an 'eyes' reaction; polling these via the reactions API is the correct way to detect review state.
The workflow pattern is: fix → fmt → focused tests → spec conformance → proptest → insta → xtask validate → commit → push → reply/resolve threads → trigger next pass.
cargo +nightly fmt --all must be run before committing; --check is used in CI validation.

friction: Codex review passes take ~8+ minutes, requiring a polling loop with 120-second sleeps to detect completion.
Two full fix-commit-push-reply-trigger cycles were needed before reaching a clean state, meaning multiple rounds of latency-heavy waiting.
The reply+resolve script required careful threading of both comment database IDs and GraphQL thread IDs, which are different identifiers.
open_items: A third @codex review pass was triggered at the end of the transcript but its outcome (approval or further findings) is not captured — the loop may need another iteration.
The commit was made with --no-gpg-sign, which may need to be revisited if repo policy requires signed commits.
